2024-04-26.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress
2024-04-26.exit_case: none — ADVANCE Act still pending in Congress, not yet passed or signed
2024-04-26.assessment: Entry week. The ADVANCE Act streamlines NRC licensing/fees for advanced reactors and is advancing with bipartisan support — a specific, datable legislative catalyst still PENDING (not yet floor-voted or signed). SMR (NuScale) is the cleanest pure-play as the only US SMR developer with an NRC-approved design; LEU (Centrus, enrichment) and CCJ (Cameco, uranium) are valid same-catalyst peers benefiting from the nuclear buildout. AI data-center power scramble reinforces the demand backdrop. Concrete next step (Senate/House passage) remains ahead; not yet mainstream-saturated. Hold with high conviction.
2024-04-26.exit_advice: Hold — bipartisan nuclear licensing bill still advancing, concrete vote/signing catalyst ahead, and AI-datacenter power demand adds a second leg. Under-the-radar entry.

2024-08-02.exit_case: none — auction results just published, capacity price surge fresh and driving generator revenue re-rating
2024-08-02.assessment: PJM's 2025/26 base residual auction cleared ~9x higher YoY, directly lifting revenue for nuclear/baseload generators CEG and VST. Specific, datable, thesis-driven shock still early. Both are clean pure-plays on merchant nuclear capacity revenue.

2024-08-23.exit_case: none — only a preliminary, non-binding takeover offer disclosed; no deal signed, rejected, or closed

This is a specific, datable, still-early M&A catalyst — the offer is preliminary and non-binding, so the arc (board reaction, price revision, Japanese regulatory/FDI scrutiny, potential competing bids) is entirely ahead. SVNDY is the clean pure-play target vehicle. Catalyst pending, edge fresh.

2024-08-23.thesis: Niger junta backs Global Atomic's Dasa uranium project
2024-08-23.assessment: Entry week. Niger's junta publicly backed Global Atomic's Dasa uranium project, sending GLATF higher on Toronto — a specific, under-the-radar geopolitical catalyst for a small-cap pure-play uranium developer. The junta's support de-risks a key asset amid a tight uranium market and Sahel political instability, with financing/permitting milestones still pending ahead. Not resolved — this is initial political backing, not a closed deal. Remaining news is generic index/market noise, irrelevant to the thesis. GLATF remains the clean pure-play vehicle.
2024-08-23.exit_advice: Hold — junta backing for Dasa is fresh; project financing and uranium supply-shock arc still ahead.
